UML:TaggedValue.value: Calibration and validation of remote sensing data and derived products — Part 3: Optical sensors
Class that contains information related to optical data radiometric calibration
The parameters name of radiometric correction
The parameters value of radiometric correction
The description of the processing procedure of radiometric correction
Information related to optical data radiometric calibration
Data type describing optical video products derived from video-mode satellite imaging, including attributes for frame rate, video duration, and motion analysis type.
Distance between the two imaging positions used to form the stereo or video pair, expressed in kilometres
Imaging mode used during video data acquisition, such as geostationary or low Earth orbit video
Type of motion analysis applied to the video product, such as object tracking or optical flow
Number of image frames captured per second during video acquisition
Total duration of the video sequence, expressed in seconds
Minimum time interval between consecutive frames in the video sequence, expressed in seconds
Describes video imaging acquired from geostationary orbit, emphasizing long-duration continuous observation of a fixed region.
Class describing the validation of optical stereo product
The dimension of the SAR multi-dimensional product
Description of the characteristics of SAR multi-dimensional product
Description of the source data from which the product is derived
Description of the multi-dimensional products validation
Reference to the parent hybrid optical product class that the stereo optical product specialises
Reference to the parent hybrid optical product class that the video optical product specialises
Abstract class defining common analysis attributes shared by numerical, categorical, and hybrid product analysis classes.
Reference to the parent analysis class that the hybrid product analysis specialises
Reference to the parent analysis class that the categorical product analysis specialises
Reference to the parent analysis class that the numerical product analysis specialises
useful information to characterize the imagery data of stereo working modes
The pixel number in the horizontal direction of a two-dimensional image
The pixel number in the vertical direction of a two-dimensional image
Data format of the SAR image according to which users can read the image
Imagery data resolution in the range direction
Imagery data resolution in the azimuth direction
Pixel spacing in the horizontal direction of a two-dimensional image
Pixel spacing in the vertical direction of a two-dimensional image
Information related to the platform orbit used for data processing
Class that contains information related to SAR data quality evaluation index
The name of SAR data validation index
Evaluation method of the index defined by indexName
Evaluation result of the index defined by indexName
Analysis method of the evaluation results
Reference to the optical data validation result associated with this direct validation activity
Class describing optical stereo products derived from multi-view stereoscopic imaging, providing geometric reconstruction of the Earth surface.
Data type defining parameters for cross-reference data acquisition used in cross-validation of optical data products.
Identifier or name of the reference product used for cross-validation
Minimum accuracy requirement for the cross-reference data used in validation, expressed in the relevant unit of measurement
Information related to calibration of Optical data
Information related to SAR data characteristic parameters calibration
Information related to calibration of SAR data
Information related to optical data characteristic parameters calibration
Information related to optical data geometric calibration
Data type that defines information about image registration, including the transformation model, control points, accuracy, and residual statistics.
Mathematical model applied for geometric transformation (e.g., affine, polynomial, RPC).
Method used for image registration (e.g., feature-based, intensity-based, DEM-assisted).
Total number of control points used in the registration process.
DataType that defines the acquisition parameters of optical data
Wave band of the SAR sensor
Centre frequency of the SAR sensor
Polarization mode of the SAR sensor
The pointing direction of the antenna
Imaging mode used by SAR sensor to collect data
The beam ID of the observaiton
The incident angle of the near range
The incident angle of the far range
Class describing the validation of optical video products derived from video-mode satellite imaging sensors.
Code list enumerating the categories of categorical optical data products, such as land cover, vegetation type, and change detection maps.
Land cover or land use classification scheme applied to the categorical product
Subtype classification within the categorical product domain, providing finer thematic distinction
Spatial mask defining the valid domain area for the categorical product classification

Class describing numerical optical data products representing continuous physical quantities derived from optical sensor measurements, such as reflectance, vegetation indices, and land surface temperature.
Category of the numerical product as defined by CA_NumProdCategoryCode, such as vegetation, atmospheric, or geometric
Ground sampling distance or spatial resolution of the numerical product, expressed in metres
Temporal resolution of the numerical product, expressed in days, representing the revisit interval
Number of spectral bands included in the numerical product
Textual description of the numerical product, including its physical meaning and derivation method
Physical unit of the numerical product values, such as reflectance (dimensionless), temperature (K), or radiance (W/m²/sr/μm)
Data type describing along-track stereo imaging acquired during a single orbital pass, using forward and backward viewing cameras or time-separated captures.
Off-nadir angle of the forward-looking view
Off-nadir angle of the backward-looking view
Time interval between forward and backward acquisitions
Satellite ground-track velocity during stereo acquisition (m/s).
Percentage of ground area overlap between the two stereo images (%).
Angular stability of the platform during the stereo sequence
Data type defining parameters for in situ measurements used as reference data in the validation of optical data products.
Method used to perform the in situ measurement, as defined by CA_MeasurementMethodCode
Abstract class describing hybrid optical products derived from multi-view or multi-temporal observations, encompassing stereo and video-derived products.
Number of distinct viewing angles or temporal acquisitions used to generate the hybrid product
Ground sampling distance of the hybrid product, expressed in metres
Time interval between successive acquisitions used to generate the hybrid product, expressed in seconds
Imaging acquisition mode used to collect the hybrid optical data, such as along-track stereo or video
Type of hybrid optical product, such as DSM, DOM, stereo pair, or video sequence
Describes video imaging acquired from non-geostationary (typically LEO) platforms, focusing on short-duration dynamic target observation.
Effective video coverage time over target (s).
Indicates if onboard motion compensation is enabled.
Platform attitude fluctuation during video acquisition
Change in off-nadir angle during sequence.
Whether simultaneous still imagery was collected.
Class that contains information related to optical data geometric calibration
The location model used for the pixel geographic location of SAR imagery data
The parameters name of the location model
The parameters value of the location model
The map projection model used for the geocoding of SAR imagery data
The resample method used in the geocoding procedure
Flag indicating that whether the terrain correction is performed
The source of DEM data used for terrain correction
Class that contains information related to optical data spectral calibration
The scattering model used for the bio-physical parameters inversion
The parameters name of the scattering model
The parameters value of the scattering model
The calibration method of the scattering model parameters
Data type describing cross-track stereo imaging acquired from different orbital passes or different satellites, using distinct viewing geometries.
Azimuth angle of the right-looking image
Number of orbital passes between the two acquisitions.
Incidence angle of the left image in a cross-track stereo pair, expressed in degrees from nadir
Azimuth angle of the left-looking image
Incidence angle of the right image
Difference in solar illumination angle between the two acquisitions
Code list enumerating the categories of numerical optical data products, such as geometric parameters, radiation, vegetation indices, and atmospheric products.
Category for numerical products representing vegetation parameters, such as NDVI, LAI, or fractional vegetation cover
Category for numerical products related to radiative quantities, such as surface reflectance, albedo, or land surface temperature
Category for numerical products representing geometric surface parameters, such as slope, aspect, or surface roughness
Category for numerical products related to water body parameters, such as turbidity, chlorophyll concentration, or water extent
Category for numerical products representing atmospheric parameters, such as aerosol optical depth or water vapour content
Category for numerical products related to cryospheric variables, such as snow cover, sea ice extent, or glacier mass
Category for numerical products derived from video-mode optical imagery
Data type describing stereo optical products derived from along-track or cross-track stereoscopic imaging configurations.
Angle between the two viewing directions of a stereo pair, expressed in degrees
Distance between the two imaging positions forming the stereo pair, expressed in kilometres
Stereo acquisition mode, such as along-track stereo or cross-track stereo
Vertical accuracy of the stereo-derived elevation product, expressed as RMSE in metres
Algorithm or method used for stereo image matching, such as area-based or feature-based matching
Type of elevation product derived from stereo imagery, such as DSM, DTM, or DOM
Class describing categorical optical data products that classify Earth surface phenomena into discrete thematic categories.
Thematic category of the categorical product as defined by CA_CatProdCategoryCode
Ground sampling distance of the categorical product, expressed in metres
Temporal resolution of the categorical product, expressed in days
Number of spectral bands used as input for the categorical product classification
Classification scheme or coding system used to assign categories to the product, such as FAO LCCS or CORINE
Textual description of the categorical product and its thematic content
Information related to validation of Optical data
The number of quality evaluation indexes of SAR data
Summary of data quality assessments performed on the optical data, including identified anomalies or quality flags
Precision of the elevation component derived from the optical data, expressed as RMSE in metres
Precision of the image matching process applied to the optical data, expressed in pixels or metres
Planimetric precision of the optical data, expressed as RMSE in metres
Description of the methodology used to assess the geometric precision of the optical data
Spectral resolution of the optical sensor, expressed in nanometres
Assessment of temporal consistency between acquisitions in multi-temporal optical data products
Temporal resolution of the optical data product, expressed in days
Information related to optical data quality evaluation index
Reference to the optical data validation result associated with this cross-validation activity
Information related to validation of SAR data
Class defining analysis metrics for numerical optical data products, including RMSE, bias, correlation, and band-specific statistical measures.
Root mean square error between the numerical product values and reference measurements
Spectral angle distance measuring the spectral similarity between the product and reference spectra
Pearson correlation coefficient between the numerical product values and reference measurements
Slope of the linear regression between the numerical product values and reference measurements
Mean difference between the estimated numerical product values and reference measurements, indicating systematic error
Class that contains information related to optical data characteristic parameters calibration
The scattering model used for the bio-physical parameters inversion
The parameters name of the scattering model
The parameters value of the scattering model
The calibration method of the scattering model parameters
Class describing the plan for validation of optical data products, including reference data strategy, validation methods, and quality metrics to be applied.
Unique identifier for the validation plan
Textual description of the validation plan, including its scope, objectives, and methodology
Class describing the details of Cal/Val of optical data
Information related to validation of SAR data
Useful information to characterize the general imagery data, e.g. panchromatic /RGB/multi-spectral/hyper-spectral data.
Quality indicator or representative value for the panchromatic data channel of the optical imagery
Quality indicator or representative value for the RGB data channels of the optical imagery
Quality indicator or representative value for the multispectral data channels of the optical imagery
Quality indicator or representative value for the hyperspectral data channels of the optical imagery
Class that contains information related to optical data quality evaluation index
The name of SAR data validation index
Evaluation method of the index defined by indexName
Evaluation result of the index defined by indexName
Analysis method of the evaluation results
Data type that defines processing parameters details of SAR data
The processing level of SAR imagery data
The processing time of SAR imagery data
The processing facility of SAR imagery data
The description of SAR imagery data processing procudure
Class describing cross-validation methods for optical data products, using reference data derived from benchmark or independently validated products.
Description of the cross-validation approach, including the reference sensor and comparison methodology
Name or identifier of the reference sensor used for cross-validation
Number of samples used in the cross-validation analysis
Flag indicating whether temporal matching was applied between the product and reference sensor acquisitions
TOPS (Terrain Observation with Progressive Scans) mode of SAR sensors
other working modes of SAR sensors
Flag or parameter indicating whether the optical imagery was acquired in scan mode
Flag or parameter indicating whether the optical imagery was acquired in stereo mode
Flag or parameter indicating whether the optical imagery was acquired in mosaic mode
Flag or parameter indicating whether the optical imagery was acquired in video mode
Class defining analysis metrics specific to hybrid optical products, including elevation accuracy, geometric consistency, and temporal consistency.
Root mean square error of elevation values in the hybrid product compared to reference data, expressed in metres
Precision of the image matching process used in stereo reconstruction or video analysis, expressed in pixels
Measure of geometric consistency across multiple views or acquisitions used in the hybrid product
Degree of temporal consistency between acquisitions in a multi-temporal or video-derived hybrid product
Data type that defines parameters for optical video-mode imaging from space platforms.
Flag indicating whether target tracking analysis was applied to the video imagery during Cal/Val
Data type defining parameters for reference data acquisition, including the type and source of reference data used in Cal/Val activities.
Date and time of the reference data acquisition used in the validation activity
Type of reference data acquisition method as defined by CA_RefTypeCode, such as in situ measurement or cross-reference
DataType that defines the information of the platform of SAR sensors
Identification that the orbit is ascending or descending
Description of the source of the orbit data
Class defining analysis metrics for categorical optical data products, including confusion matrix, overall accuracy, Kappa coefficient, and precision/recall.
Precision of the classification for each category, defined as the ratio of true positives to all predicted positives
Recall of the classification for each category, defined as the ratio of true positives to all actual positives
Confusion matrix summarising the agreement between classified categories and reference data
Producer's accuracy for each class, indicating the probability that a reference sample is correctly classified
User's accuracy for each class, indicating the probability that a classified pixel actually belongs to that class
Commission error rate of the categorical product, indicating the proportion of incorrectly included pixels per class
Omission error rate of the categorical product, indicating the proportion of incorrectly excluded pixels per class
Overall classification accuracy expressed as the ratio of correctly classified samples to total samples
Kappa coefficient measuring agreement between the classified product and reference data, corrected for chance
Useful information to characterize the imagery data of stereo optical mode.
Distance between imaging positions forming the stereo pair (m).
Angle between the viewing directions of the stereo pair
Ground sampling distance of each stereo image
Expected vertical accuracy derived from stereo geometry
Type of stereo configuration (e.g., along-track, cross-track, tri-stereo).
Calibration and validation of remote sensing data and derived products — Part 3: Optical sensors
false#NOTES#Values: true,false Default: false Description: Identifies the class as a collection
false#NOTES#Values: true,false Default: false Description: Suppress creation of a named complex type ( property type ) that supports inline or byReference encoding
false#NOTES#Values: true,false Default: false Description: If 'false', the class will be mapped to an XSDsimpleType. Else, it will be exported as an external dictionary.
#NOTES#Description: Default value for the URI representing the dictionary
false#NOTES#Values: true,false Default: false Description: Identifies the class as a collection
false#NOTES#Values: true,false Default: false Description: Suppress creation of a named complex type ( property type ) that supports inline or byReference encoding
false#NOTES#Values: true,false Default: false Description: Identifies the class as a collection
false#NOTES#Values: true,false Default: false Description: Suppress creation of a named complex type ( property type ) that supports inline or byReference encoding
false#NOTES#Values: true,false Default: false Description: Identifies the class as a collection
false#NOTES#Values: true,false Default: false Description: Suppress creation of a named complex type ( property type ) that supports inline or byReference encoding
false#NOTES#Values: true,false Default: false Description: If 'false', the class will be mapped to an XSDsimpleType. Else, it will be exported as an external dictionary.
#NOTES#Description: Default value for the URI representing the dictionary
false#NOTES#Values: true,false Default: false Description: If 'false', the class will be mapped to an XSDsimpleType. Else, it will be exported as an external dictionary.
#NOTES#Description: Default value for the URI representing the dictionary
false#NOTES#Values: true,false Default: false Description: Identifies the class as a collection
false#NOTES#Values: true,false Default: false Description: Suppress creation of a named complex type ( property type ) that supports inline or byReference encoding
false#NOTES#Values: true,false Default: false Description: Identifies the class as a collection
false#NOTES#Values: true,false Default: false Description: Suppress creation of a named complex type ( property type ) that supports inline or byReference encoding
false#NOTES#Values: true,false Default: false Description: If 'false', the class will be mapped to an XSDsimpleType. Else, it will be exported as an external dictionary.
#NOTES#Description: Default value for the URI representing the dictionary

UML:Diagram.name: Fig 1 - Overview of ISO 19124-3 Package Structure
Fig 2 - Optical Data CalVal and Calibration Classes
Fig 4 - Optical Imagery CalVal and Acquisition Parameters
Fig 6 - Direct and Cross Validation Methods
Fig 8 - Categorical Product and Category Code
Fig 7 - Numerical Product and Category Code
Fig 10 - Validation Plan and Reference Data Acquisition
Fig 9 - Hybrid Stereo and Video Product Classes
